[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m# Definition of done[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                              [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mThe task is complete only when committed on your branch.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                          [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mWhen you believe it is complete, append `done: {summary}` to the status file and stop.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                            [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mFirstmate will then instruct you to run /no-mistakes to validate and ship a PR.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                   [0m
[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                                    [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mYou drive no-mistakes by responding to its gates, not by implementing fixes.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                      [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mFollow the guidance no-mistakes itself provides for the mechanics: it loads when you invoke /no-mistakes, and `no-mistakes axi run --help` plus the `help` lines in each `axi` response are authoritative and [0m[48;5;8m    [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mversion-matched to the installed binary.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                          [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mDo not hand-edit, commit, or fix findings yourself while a run is active - the pipeline applies every fix.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                        [0m
[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                                    [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mTwo firstmate-specific rules layer on top of that guidance:[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                       [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- ask-user findings are not yours to answer: escalate to firstmate (rule 6) and stop.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                             [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m  When the decision comes back, feed it to the gate with `no-mistakes axi respond` and let the pipeline apply it - do not route the question to "the user" or implement the fix yourself.[0m[48;5;8m                         [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- Avoid `--yes`: the captain, not you, owns the ask-user decisions it would silently auto-resolve.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                [0m
[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                                    [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mAfter /no-mistakes reports CI green, append `done: PR {url} checks green` and stop. You are finished.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                             [0m

24.ansi:
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m`.seen-<id>_<type>`, `.stale-<project>_fm-<id>`.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                  [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **Backend abstraction:** runtime endpoints go through `bin/fm-backend.sh` (`fm_backend_kill`, `fm_backend_target`, etc.). The primary backends are `tmux` (verified reference) and `herdr` (experimental).[0m[48;5;8m      [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **Process cleanup:** kill children of a task's process group (`kill -- -$PGID`) before killing the runtime endpoint. Always check `$self_pgid` against the target PGID first.[0m[48;5;8m                                   [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **Meta files:** `state/<id>.meta` is the durable task record. Read with `fm_meta_get <file> <key>`, write with the `key=value` line format.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                     [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **All path references in scripts should be absolute** (use `pwd -P` and `cd` into dirs before resolving paths).[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                 [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- Sharp edges: - **`kill` is a bash built-in** — a fakebin `kill` mock will NOT shadow the built-in. Test process-killing code via diagnostic stderr output or by verifying real process behavior, never via kill[0m[48;5;8m [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mmocks in PATH.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                    [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **Process group kill (`kill -- -$PGID`) destroys the entire group.** If the PGID matches the current shell's PGID, the script kills itself and its parent session. Always guard with `[ "$pgid" != "$self_pgid"[0m[48;5;8m [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m]`.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                               [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **`tmux list-panes -t "$T"` fails** when the window is already gone (e.g., another process killed it). Always use `|| true` on these calls and check for empty output.[0m[48;5;8m                                          [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **`treehouse return --force` also kills processes** in the worktree before resetting it. Killing the process group of the tmux pane first prevents orphans from being reparented to PID 1.[0m[48;5;8m                      [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **Scratch worktrees** (`~/.treehouse/scratch-project-*`) are managed by treehouse for transient tests but are NOT listed in `treehouse status`. Only the 8 numbered pool slots are tracked.[0m[48;5;8m                     [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8m- **`ps -eo pid=,ppid=,command=`** output format differs between macOS (BSD ps) and Linux. The command column on macOS is truncated at the process name in `ps -o comm`; use `command` (full argv) for matching.[0m[48;5;8m  [0m
[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                                                                                                    [0m

[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mThis is a SCOUT task: the deliverable is a written report, not a PR.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                                              [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mThe worktree is your laboratory - install, run, edit, and make scratch commits freely; all of it is discarded at teardown.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                        [0m
[0m[48;5;8m  [0m[38;5;15m[48;5;8mThe report is the only thing that survives, so anything worth keeping must be in it.[0m[48;5;8m                                                                                                                              [0m
